check.command: powershell -NoProfile -Command "Import-Module WebAdministration -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; $value=(Get-WebConfigurationProperty -PSPath 'IIS:\' -Filter '/system.ftpServer/security/authentication/anonymousAuthentication' -Name enabled -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Value; if ($value -eq $false) { 'Disabled' }"

candidate_check.description: Windows Server 2019 FTP servers must be configured to prevent anonymous logons.

check_content_excerpt:
If FTP is not installed on the system, this is NA.

Open "Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager".

Select the server.

Double-click "FTP Authentication".

If the "Anonymous Authentication" status is "Enabled", this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Configure the FTP service to prevent anonymous logons.

Open "Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager".

Select the server.

Double-click "FTP Authentication".

Select "Anonymous Authentication".

Select "Disabled" under "Actions".
title: Windows Server 2019 FTP servers must be configured to prevent anonymous logons.
